title: Outcomes of Liver Transplant Versus Partial Hepatectomy for Perihilar Cholangiocarcinoma Patients Requiring Arterial Reconstruction.
abstract: Liver resection (LR) and orthotopic liver transplantation (OLT) are therapeutic options for locally advanced perihilar cholangiocarcinoma (pCCA) requiring hepatic artery reconstruction (HAR). This study aimed to compare short- and long-term outcomes of LR and OLT. Outcomes were major vascular complications, 90-day mortality, overall survival (OS) and recurrence-free survival (RFS).
